message:
feat(fp-types): period_range builder (br-2jef / epoj Phase 2)

Closes br-frankenpandas-2jef. Adds count-based period_range matching pd.period_range(start, periods=N) for the ordinal-arithmetic case. Pure additive — no chrono dep yet, calendar arithmetic stays in epoj Phase 3.

- pub fn period_range(start: Period, periods: usize) -> Vec<Period>: builds N consecutive periods starting at start.ordinal, via the existing Period::shift saturating-arithmetic path. Frequency inherits from start (no redundant freq parameter).
- Empty input (periods=0) returns empty Vec.

Doctest in the rustdoc + 6 unit tests cover: zero periods, single period, sequential ordinal increment, frequency preserved across all positions, negative starting ordinal (pre-epoch periods are valid on the signed i64 axis), large-N (1024 monthly periods) allocation sanity.

rch: cargo test -p fp-types --lib period_range (6 pass), cargo check --workspace --all-targets clean, cargo clippy -p fp-types --all-targets -D warnings clean, ubs exit 0.

Phase 3 (calendar arithmetic + start/end string-anchored period_range like pd.period_range('2024Q1', '2024Q4', freq='Q')) deferred and lands when chrono integration arrives.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"frankenpandas-1d9y","title":"[HIGH] infra: rust-toolchain.toml pins channel=nightly with no date — CI and local drift silently across nightly releases","description":"Toolchain-pin audit on rust-toolchain.toml + build reproducibility.

**Observation.** rust-toolchain.toml has `channel = "nightly"` with no date pin. No `components` field. No `targets` field.

**Consequence.**
- A developer who installs the repo today gets nightly-2026-04-22.
- A week later, nightly advances to nightly-2026-04-29. Their `cargo build` silently upgrades.
- A breaking change in a std library, rustc lint, or proc-macro behavior then surfaces as a mysterious build failure that didn't affect any code — it just reproduces once on one machine.
- CI runs `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly` (ci.yml line 21) which is the SAME floating reference — CI and local can differ by whatever diff landed in nightly in between.
- rustc version doesn't appear in `cargo --version` output committed artifacts; reproducing a failing CI build locally requires manually matching the nightly date.

**Real example this year:** in nightly-2026-03-28, the `#[must_use]` lint on `Result` tightened; hundreds of Rust libraries had to add `let _ =` prefixes. If our build broke on that nightly, a developer who pulled in the previous week would see green while a fresh clone would see red.

**Severity.** HIGH — build reproducibility is a blocker for:
1. Bisecting a conformance regression (which nightly introduced the divergence?)
2. Onboarding a new contributor (their nightly breaks, ours builds)
3. Deterministic binary distribution (the planned PyO3 wheel-build would produce different bytes depending on which nightly the builder used)

**Fix shape:**
1. `rust-toolchain.toml` pins the exact nightly date:
     [toolchain]
     channel = "nightly-2026-04-22"
     components = ["rustfmt", "clippy", "rust-src"]
2. Bump ritual: same cadence as the existing lib-updater sweep (see UPGRADE_LOG.md). Pin → sweep → test → commit. Monthly or on-demand.
3. CI uses `dtolnay/rust-toolchain@{{ rust-toolchain.toml channel }}` via the `toolchain` input so CI honors the pin rather than floating.
4. Document the pinning policy in AGENTS.md near the build-environment section.

Blast radius: rust-toolchain.toml (1 line change), ci.yml (1 line change), AGENTS.md (+1 paragraph). Zero source code changes.

This is a sibling issue to br-frankenpandas-8irq (pandas version pin) + br-frankenpandas-boyr (fixture pandas-version provenance). Same class: pin the reference, document the update ritual.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T19:28:53.722920769Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T23:13:37.776387738Z","close_reason":"Pinned the Rust nightly date in rust-toolchain.toml, taught CI to resolve that pin from the toolchain file, and documented the policy in AGENTS.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["infra","reproducibility","review","toolchain"]}

+{"id":"frankenpandas-2jef","title":"fp-types period_range builder (epoj Phase 2)","description":"Phase 2 follow-up to br-epoj (Period scaffolding shipped in commit b646d88).

**Scope.** Add `pub fn period_range(start, periods)` to fp-types matching `pd.period_range(start, periods=N, freq=...)` for the count-based form. Returns N consecutive periods starting at `start`.

**Reference behavior (pandas 2.2):**
- `pd.period_range(start='2024Q1', periods=4, freq='Q')` → `PeriodIndex(['2024Q1', '2024Q2', '2024Q3', '2024Q4'])`.
- `periods=0` → empty PeriodIndex.
- Frequency comes from the start Period itself (don't redundantly pass it).

**FrankenPandas state.** Phase 1 shipped Period struct + PeriodFreq enum + shift / diff / cmp_same_freq. No range builder. Pure ordinal arithmetic — no chrono needed in Phase 2.

**Fix shape (purely additive in fp-types):**
- `pub fn period_range(start: Period, periods: usize) -> Vec<Period>` — N periods starting from `start.ordinal`.
- 6 unit tests: zero periods, one period, sequential ordinals, freq preserved, large N, end-vs-start (start<>end variants live in Phase 3 once calendar arithmetic lands).

**Blast radius.** Purely additive in fp-types. Zero call-site touches. fp-types stays chrono-free.

**Severity.** LOW — Period is foundational scaffolding; range builder is the natural next step.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:24:37.112194224Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:27:03.510628198Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:27:03.508126922Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dtype","parity-gap","period","review"]}

@@ -351,6 +353,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-hg60","title":"[HIGH] security: no secret scanning in CI or pre-commit — leaked credentials in commits become permanent git history","description":"Secret / credential hygiene audit.

**Observation.**
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml`: no gitleaks, no truffleHog, no detect-secrets, no similar scanner.
- Repo has ONE pre-commit hook: `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/50-agent-mail.py` (coordination for the multi-agent swarm, not a secret scan).
- No `.pre-commit-config.yaml` (the pre-commit.com framework) — so no ecosystem-standard secret hooks either.
- No `.gitallowed` / `.gitleaksignore` / `.git-crypt` / `.gitattributes` with secret-relevant entries — nothing that suggests prior secret-scan discipline.

**Context.** This is a public-library repo that will ship to crates.io (once br-4clx lands). The multi-agent swarm produces 20+ commits/day from multiple authors. Real surface areas where secrets leak:
- CI artifacts: a test or fixture that inadvertently embeds a token (env var leaked into a string literal).
- Examples in rustdoc (doctests) pulling from real services with hardcoded API keys for demo purposes.
- Oracle script `oracle/pandas_oracle.py`: if it ever calls a remote service (it currently doesn't, but nothing prevents a PR adding one with a key).
- Sidecar config in `artifacts/`: JSON reports with paths, usernames, or host identifiers that constitute PII/infra info.

**Severity.** HIGH — git history is append-only for practical purposes. A secret committed today is discoverable by any scraper forever. Cleanup requires force-pushes (destructive, see skill rules about confirmations) plus rotation of every leaked credential.

**Fix shape:**

1. CI job `security`: runs gitleaks on every PR and on the `main` branch tip. Fails CI on any detection.
   ```yaml
   - uses: gitleaks/gitleaks-action@v2
     env:
       GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
   ```
   Alternatively `trufflehog` for broader pattern coverage. Either is one step (~15s runtime).

2. Pre-commit hook `.git/hooks/hooks.d/pre-commit/20-secret-scan.sh`:
   - Runs `gitleaks protect --staged` on every commit.
   - Exits non-zero on finding.
   - Extends the existing 50-agent-mail.py single-hook pattern. Use numeric prefix ordering so secret scan runs before agent-mail (fast-fail on credentials before coordinating the commit with other agents).

3. `.gitleaks.toml` config:
   - Allowlist obviously-non-secret patterns (fixture data, example API URLs, placeholder strings).
   - Tighten rulesets for AWS / GCP / GitHub / npm / Docker Hub / Slack webhook patterns.

4. Documentation in CONTRIBUTING.md (br-6d5s): "Never commit real credentials. Use env vars for CI secrets via GitHub Actions secrets. All test fixtures must use placeholder tokens starting with `test_` or `fake_`."

5. One-time retroactive scan: run gitleaks against the entire repo history NOW (before the gate goes live) so any prior leaks can be rotated.

Blast radius: 1 new CI job, 1 new pre-commit hook, 1 new .gitleaks.toml, 1 section in CONTRIBUTING.md. Zero source code changes beyond any secrets that the retroactive scan surfaces.

Related:
- br-8k1i (SECURITY.md) — disclosure policy for external reports; this bead prevents us leaking secrets of our own.
- br-6d5s (CONTRIBUTING.md) — where the documented policy lives.
- br-36qc (cargo-audit) — detects vulns in deps; this bead detects secrets in our own commits.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:40:36.501917176Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-23T16:43:52.952767584Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T16:43:52.950256132Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["pre-commit","review","secrets","security"]}

 {"id":"frankenpandas-hnf3","title":"fp-columnar Column.concat (binary concat) parity","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T06:29:34.523095365Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:33:59.676470628Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:33:59.674649871Z","close_reason":"Implemented Column::concat in commit 593b679, 2 tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-ho6t","title":"[REALITY-CHECK HIGH] proptest prop_excel_round_trip_preserves_shape FAILS — public default round-trip broken","description":"rch exec cargo test --workspace exposed prop_excel_round_trip_preserves_shape at crates/fp-conformance/tests/proptest_properties.rs:9746 FAILS with Excel round-trip must preserve column count: actual=2 expected=1. Same root cause as br-989b/br-mxho — fp_io::write_excel_bytes default index=true emits row index as leading column; fp_io::read_excel_bytes default does not promote it back. Default-pair round-trip leaks an extra column. Conformance harness was patched but public defaults still broken AND proptest catches it. Validates LOW arnn green-for-the-wrong-reason and ESCALATES to HIGH. Fix: make read_excel_bytes default detect-and-strip leading index column OR change write default to index=false (diverges from pandas; needs sign-off). Add fp-io level default-pair round-trip test.","notes":"cc-pandas triage 2026-04-22: fixed in-tree via commit d0c5b2f. fp-io added infer_writer_emitted_default_excel_index_col which detects the writer-emitted default leading RangeIndex column (header auto-generated + values 0,1,2,... matching row position) and strips it back to the DataFrame index during read_excel_bytes. prop_excel_default_round_trip_preserves_shape flipped to assert shape preservation (no more +1 extra column). Also retires the green-for-wrong-reason state arnn flagged: assert_excel_roundtrip now uses write_excel_bytes default + read_excel_bytes default and passes. Tests: 2/2 fp-io excel_default_* unit tests pass (excel_default_read_promotes_writer_range_index_back_to_index + excel_default_read_keeps_non_range_generated_leading_column_as_data); 3/3 excel proptests pass (prop_excel_default_round_trip_preserves_shape + prop_excel_round_trip_preserves_shape_when_index_false + prop_excel_round_trip_int64_exact).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:22:50.499192351Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:20.922251258Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:30:20.919425175Z","closed_by_session":"cc-pandas-ho6t","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
